title: C Goto Statement
content:
### Goto Statement in C

The `goto` statement transfers control to a labeled statement elsewhere in the same function.

### Syntax

```c
goto label;
// ...
label:
  // code
```

### Label Rules

| Rule | Description |
|------|-------------|
| Label name | Follows identifier naming rules |
| Placement | Before the statement it marks |
| Scope | Same function only (not cross-function) |
| Colon | Required after label name |

### When Goto Is Acceptable

- Breaking out of deeply nested loops (multiple levels)
- Error cleanup in functions (single exit point)
- State machine implementations

### Why Avoid Goto

- Creates spaghetti code (unstructured, hard to follow)
- Makes debugging and maintenance difficult
- Can skip variable initialization
- Structured alternatives exist (break, continue, functions)

---

> **Key Point:** In C, `goto` is occasionally useful for breaking out of nested loops or centralized error handling, but it should be used sparingly.
